purpose: Complete the missing hard retrieval layer around CodeCompass/rag-helper so Ananta can use structured CodeCompass outputs through lexical FTS/BM25, structured vector search, graph expansion and relation-aware context assembly before delegating work to opencode, ananta-worker or other execution backends.

already_present: Hybrid context execution path via sgpt /execute with use_hybrid_context=true.
ContextManagerService builds grounded prompts before execution backends run.
RagService facade delegates retrieval and context bundle assembly.
RetrievalService already fuses repository_map, semantic_search, agentic_search, knowledge_index and result_memory chunks.
KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService reads CodeCompass index.jsonl, details.jsonl and relations.jsonl.
KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService has custom lexical scoring with symbol, path, relation, summary, content and focus weights.
SemanticSearchEngine builds a LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex over configured data roots.
AgenticSearchEngine provides rg-based live lexical search.
missing_or_incomplete: No persistent FTS/BM25 index over CodeCompass records found.
No dedicated structured vector store over embedding.jsonl records found.
No graph traversal over graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl found.
relations.jsonl is scored as records but not yet used as executable graph expansion edges.
No explicit retrieval benchmark comparing current lexical scoring, FTS/BM25, vectors and graph expansion found.

non_goals: Do not replace the existing ContextManagerService/RagService/RetrievalService chain.
Do not force OpenCode or ananta-worker to do retrieval themselves.
Do not make external vector DBs mandatory for local-dev startup.
Do not remove the current custom lexical KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService scoring until the new engines are benchmarked.
Do not expose raw secrets or large unredacted code blobs in context bundles.
success_criteria: CodeCompass outputs can be indexed into a persistent lexical FTS/BM25 store.
embedding.jsonl can be indexed as structured vector records with id, kind, file, role labels and provenance metadata preserved.
graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl can be loaded and traversed for 1-hop and bounded 2-hop context expansion.
relations.jsonl can drive targeted expansions such as parent type, child method, calls_probable_target, injects_dependency, JPA relations and transactional boundaries.
RetrievalService can fuse lexical, vector, graph, relation and existing engines with traceable scores.
Context bundles include why-this-context, source provenance and expansion trace.
Execution backends receive compact grounded prompts; retrieval stays hub/context-layer owned.
Benchmarks prove whether each new engine improves bugfix, refactor, architecture and config retrieval tasks.

tasks.title: Document current retrieval flow
acceptance_criteria: Document the current path: sgpt route -> ContextManagerService -> RagService -> RetrievalService -> ContextBundleService -> backend.
List current engines: repository_map, semantic_search, agentic_search, knowledge_index, result_memory.
Describe exactly what KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService reads today: index.jsonl, details.jsonl, relations.jsonl.
State clearly that current knowledge_index search is lexical/scored but not a persistent FTS/BM25 index.
State clearly that graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl are not yet used for traversal.

tasks.title: Add retrieval engine contract
acceptance_criteria: Define provider-neutral RetrievalEngine interface with search(query, top_k, task_kind, retrieval_intent, source_scopes).
Return existing ContextChunk-compatible payloads with engine, source, content, score and metadata.
Contract supports preflight/status diagnostics.
No engine-specific dependencies are required to import the contract.
Tests verify a mock engine can be registered and queried.

tasks.title: Add CodeCompass output discovery helper
acceptance_criteria: Helper discovers available CodeCompass output files per completed KnowledgeIndex.
Helper recognizes index.jsonl, details.jsonl, relations.jsonl, embedding.jsonl, context.jsonl, graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl.
Helper tolerates missing optional files without failing retrieval.
Helper yields parsed JSONL records with source metadata: knowledge_index_id, artifact_id, source_scope, output_dir, record_file.
Tests cover complete output, partial output and malformed JSONL lines.

tasks.title: Implement SQLite FTS5 store for CodeCompass records
acceptance_criteria: Create or open a SQLite database under the knowledge index output or configured retrieval cache directory.
Create FTS5 table for record_id, kind, file, name, symbol_text, path_text, summary_text, content_text, relation_text, focus_text and source metadata.
Index CodeCompass records from index.jsonl, details.jsonl, context.jsonl and optionally relations.jsonl.
Use stable cache invalidation based on knowledge_index_id, output_dir, manifest hash or output file mtimes.
Do not block core startup when SQLite FTS5 is unavailable; report degraded preflight instead.
Tests verify insert, rebuild, query and cache invalidation.

tasks.title: Add BM25/FTS retrieval engine
acceptance_criteria: Engine queries SQLite FTS5 with bm25 ranking where available.
Engine returns ContextChunk objects with engine='codecompass_fts'.
Metadata includes record_id, record_kind, record_file, file, knowledge_index_id, artifact_id, source_scope and lexical_score_breakdown.
Engine boosts exact symbol/path matches for Java class/method/config/XML names.
Engine is bounded by top_k and content length limits.
Tests compare exact class/method query results against expected ranked records.

tasks.title: Wire FTS engine into RetrievalService fusion
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalService can include codecompass_fts as a retrieval engine when enabled.
Task profiles weight codecompass_fts strongly for bugfix, refactor, config and exact-symbol queries.
Existing knowledge_index lexical scoring remains available as fallback during rollout.
Fusion metadata reports engine contribution counts and codecompass_fts selected chunks.
Tests verify FTS chunks can reach final context bundle.

tasks.title: Implement structured embedding.jsonl loader
acceptance_criteria: Loader reads embedding.jsonl records and extracts id, kind, file, embedding_text, summary, role_labels, importance_score and generated_code metadata.
Loader skips records with empty embedding_text.
Loader preserves provenance back to knowledge_index_id and output_dir.
Loader supports compact deterministic document text for vector indexing.
Tests cover normal records, missing fields and malformed lines.

tasks.title: Add structured vector store for CodeCompass embeddings
acceptance_criteria: Build a persistent vector index from embedding.jsonl records rather than treating JSONL as plain text.
Preserve record metadata for retrieval and graph expansion: id, kind, file, parent_id if available, role_labels, importance_score, knowledge_index_id.
Support local-dev backend with LlamaIndex default vector store or another dependency already acceptable to the project.
Support optional provider configuration for future Ollama/LM Studio/OpenAI-compatible embedding backends without making them mandatory.
Detect stale vector indexes by output manifest/hash/mtime and rebuild safely.
Tests use a fake embedding/vector implementation where external models are not available.

tasks.title: Add CodeCompass vector retrieval engine
acceptance_criteria: Engine searches structured CodeCompass vector store and returns ContextChunk objects with engine='codecompass_vector'.
Metadata includes vector score, record_id, record_kind, role_labels, file and provenance.
Task profiles boost vector results for fuzzy semantic questions and architecture analysis.
Engine can be disabled independently if embedding backend is unavailable.
Tests verify vector result metadata is preserved into ContextChunk.

tasks.title: Implement CodeCompass graph store loader
acceptance_criteria: Load graph_nodes.jsonl and graph_edges.jsonl when present.
Also load parent_id relationships from index/details records as fallback edges.
Expose lookup by node id, file, kind and record id.
Expose outgoing_edges(node_id, edge_types), incoming_edges(node_id, edge_types), neighbors(node_id, depth, edge_types).
Bound traversal by max depth, max nodes and allowed edge types.
Tests cover parent_child, relation edges, missing graph files and cycle protection.

tasks.title: Implement relation edge normalizer from relations.jsonl
acceptance_criteria: Normalize relations.jsonl records into graph edges with source, target, type, confidence, heuristic and provenance.
Support relation keys used by CodeCompass: from, to, type, relation, target_resolved, confidence, heuristic where present.

Map important CodeCompass relation types: calls_probable_target, injects_dependency, field_type_uses, extends, implements, jpa relations, transactional_boundary, declares_bean, child_of_type, child_of_file.
Handle unresolved target_resolved values without crashing.
Tests cover resolved and unresolved relations.

tasks.title: Add graph expansion retrieval engine
acceptance_criteria: Engine accepts seed chunks from FTS/vector/knowledge_index and expands by graph neighbors.
Support expansion profiles: bugfix_local, refactor_navigation, architecture_review, config_integration.
For method seeds include parent type, child details, calls_probable_target and directly used types within limits.
For type seeds include file parent, important methods, injected dependencies, extends/implements and framework role relations.
For JPA/Spring seeds include entity/repository/bean/transactional relations when present.
Return expansion chunks with engine='codecompass_graph' and metadata.expanded_from.
Tests verify bounded 1-hop and 2-hop expansion.

tasks.title: Add relation-aware context explanations
acceptance_criteria: Generate compact why-this-context explanations for graph-expanded chunks.
Examples: selected because method calls probable target, selected because class injects dependency, selected because entity relation points here.
Explanations are included in chunk metadata and final bundle explainability.
Do not leak huge raw relation payloads into prompts.
Tests verify explanation text for core relation types.

tasks.title: Extend RetrievalService fusion for CodeCompass engines
acceptance_criteria: Fusion supports codecompass_fts, codecompass_vector and codecompass_graph engines.
Graph expansion runs after initial seed retrieval and before final rerank/diversity cut.
Task profile weights are explicit for bugfix, refactor, architecture and config tasks.
Deduplication handles same record returned by FTS, vector and graph engines.
Selection trace includes original score, fusion score, engine weights, expansion source and final rank.
Tests verify a seed result can be expanded and survive final context selection.

tasks.title: Update ContextBundleService for graph/FTS/vector provenance
acceptance_criteria: Context bundles preserve engine, source_type, record_id, relation_path and expanded_from metadata.
Context text groups related graph-expanded chunks near their seed where possible.
Token budgeting prevents graph expansion from flooding the prompt.
Bundle explainability reports FTS/vector/graph contribution counts.
Tests verify provenance is visible in bundle metadata but prompt text remains compact.

tasks.title: Add retrieval engine configuration flags
acceptance_criteria: Add flags to enable/disable codecompass_fts, codecompass_vector and codecompass_graph independently.
Default local-dev behavior must remain safe and not require external vector DB/model services.
Expose preflight diagnostics for each engine.
Tests verify disabled engines do not run and enabled-but-unavailable engines degrade cleanly.

tasks.title: Add retrieval benchmark suite
acceptance_criteria: Create small fixture with Java classes, methods, Spring/JPA relations, XML/XSD/config and expected retrieval targets.
Benchmark compares current knowledge_index scoring, FTS, vector and graph-expanded hybrid modes.
Measure hit@k, engine contribution, token estimate and explanation coverage.
Include scenarios: bugfix, refactor, architecture overview, config integration and fuzzy semantic search.
Tests are deterministic and do not require external LLM or external embedding provider.

tasks.title: Document operational rollout and migration path
acceptance_criteria: Document rollout stages: current lexical scoring, FTS on, vector on, graph expansion on, full hybrid.
Document how to rebuild CodeCompass indexes and retrieval caches.
Document fallback behavior when vector or graph engines are unavailable.
Document recommended profiles for large Java/Spring/XML/XSD projects.
Document how opencode/ananta-worker receive grounded context and why retrieval stays outside execution backends.

execution_order_summary: First document the current retrieval flow so the existing layer is not rebuilt accidentally.
Add shared engine contracts and a CodeCompass output reader.
Implement persistent FTS/BM25 over CodeCompass records and wire it as an engine.
Implement structured vector indexing over embedding.jsonl records rather than generic JSONL text ingestion.
Load graph_nodes/graph_edges and normalize relations.jsonl into traversable edges.
Add graph expansion after initial FTS/vector/knowledge_index seed retrieval.
Fuse all engines in RetrievalService with traceable scores and provenance.
Update context bundles and benchmarks before enabling broadly.
summary_notes: The existing Ananta context layer is real and should be extended, not replaced.
The current KnowledgeIndexRetrievalService is already FTS-like through custom lexical scoring, but adding SQLite FTS5/BM25 would make it faster, persistent and more standard.
The current LlamaIndex semantic engine is real, but it is not yet a structured CodeCompass embedding.jsonl vector index with preserved record metadata.
The largest missing value is graph expansion: use CodeCompass relations/nodes/edges to expand from a seed method/type/config record to its relevant neighborhood.
OpenCode and ananta-worker should remain execution backends. Retrieval and context construction should stay in the hub-owned context layer.
WCR-T30 decision (2026-04-29): keep this CHR roadmap as historical/specialized detail; no automatic promotion into parent/global todo tracks.
